query: Meghalaya Legislative Assembly party composition 2026 NPP seats MDA coalition strength
answer:
## Summary

The Meghalaya Legislative Assembly (60 seats) is currently governed by the **NPP-led MDA coalition** with a commanding supermajority. Key takeaways:

- **NPP holds 26 seats** — the single largest party by a wide margin
- **MDA coalition total: ~48–51 seats** (NPP 26 + UDP 11 + BJP 2 + HSPDP 2 + PDF 2 + supporting independents)
- The opposition is fragmented: INC has only 5 seats, and VPP has 4
- The government is highly stable with strength well above the 31-seat majority mark

**Important caveat**: Web search and fetch tools were unavailable during this session. All data is drawn from training knowledge reflecting the 2023 election results and developments through early 2025. Any 2025–2026 changes (by-elections, defections, realignments) could not be verified.

key_facts: The Meghalaya Legislative Assembly has 60 total seats; majority mark is 31.
The NPP won 26 seats in the February 2023 election, making it the single largest party (+7 from 2018).
Conrad K. Sangma (NPP) serves as Chief Minister for a second consecutive term.
The MDA coalition comprises NPP (26), UDP (11), BJP (2), HSPDP (2), PDF (2), plus supporting independents.
Estimated MDA coalition floor strength is approximately 48–51 seats out of 60, a supermajority.
The Indian National Congress was reduced to just 5 seats, its worst-ever result in Meghalaya.
The Voice of the People Party (VPP), a new entrant, won 4 seats and sits in opposition.
Independents won approximately 8 seats in the 2023 election, with several supporting the MDA.
No major defections from the MDA coalition were reported through early 2025.
Data vintage caveat: Unable to verify 2026 developments (by-elections, defections) due to tool unavailability.
